name: Goblin Warrior
summary: A small, green-skinned humanoid wielding a dogslicer and shortbow.
description: Goblins are small, aggressive creatures known for their love of fire, hatred of dogs and horses, and their distinctive chanting songs. Goblin warriors form the backbone of any goblin raiding party, wielding dogslicers — their signature weapons crafted from whatever sharp materials they can find.

name: Kobold Scout
summary: A small reptilian humanoid skilled in traps and ambushes.
description: Kobolds are small, reptilian humanoids who live in extensive tunnel networks. Kobold scouts patrol the perimeters of their warrens, using their darkvision and knowledge of the terrain to detect intruders. They prefer to soften enemies with traps and ranged attacks before closing in.

name: Skeleton Guard
summary: An animated skeleton wielding a scimitar and shield.
description: Skeleton guards are among the most common undead, created when necromantic magic reanimates a humanoid corpse stripped of flesh. Though mindless, they follow the commands of their creator with unwavering obedience, making them popular guardians for tombs and lairs.

name: Wolf
summary: A cunning predator that hunts in packs.
description: Wolves are social predators that hunt in packs, using coordinated tactics to bring down prey larger than themselves. They are commonly encountered in forests and plains, and their pack tactics ability makes them dangerous even to well-armed adventurers when they attack in numbers.

name: Zombie Shambler
summary: A slow, rotting corpse animated by necromantic energy.
description: Zombie shamblers are the most common variety of zombie — slow, mindless undead created from humanoid corpses. Though individually weak, they are dangerous in groups, as their relentless advance and grasping hands can overwhelm unprepared adventurers. They are notably resistant to slashing damage but vulnerable to vitality energy.

name: Orc Brute
summary: A fierce orc warrior relying on raw strength and aggression.
description: Orc brutes are the rank-and-file warriors of orc warbands, relying on brute strength and ferocity in battle. They favor heavy weapons and direct charges, using their Ferocity ability to fight on even after receiving what would be a killing blow to lesser creatures.

name: Giant Rat
summary: An oversized rodent common in sewers and dungeons.
description: Giant rats are oversized rodents commonly found in sewers, dungeons, and other dark places where food scraps accumulate. They are more aggressive than their smaller cousins and carry diseases that can weaken even hardy adventurers. They typically attack in swarms when cornered.

name: Ogre Warrior
summary: A towering, brutish giant that wields an oversized club.
description: Ogres are dim-witted but enormously strong giants that dwell in hills, caves, and ruins. Ogre warriors are the most common variety, using their massive size and strength to bully smaller creatures. They wield oversized weapons that deal devastating damage, making them a serious threat despite their low intelligence.

name: Troll
summary: A lanky giant with powerful regeneration, vulnerable to fire and acid.
description: Trolls are lanky, green-skinned giants known for their extraordinary regeneration. They can recover from nearly any wound, regrowing severed limbs and healing grievous injuries in seconds. Only fire and acid can suppress a troll's regeneration, making these damage types essential when fighting them. Trolls are voracious predators that eat anything they can catch.

name: Wyvern
summary: A two-legged dragon-like creature with a venomous stinger tail.
description: Wyverns are aggressive, two-legged dragon-like creatures distinguished by their venomous stinger tails. Unlike true dragons, they lack forelegs and breath weapons, relying instead on their powerful jaws, talons, and deadly poison. They are territorial predators that nest in high cliffs and mountain ledges.

name: Hill Giant
summary: A brutish giant that hurls rocks and swings massive clubs.
description: Hill giants are the most common and least intelligent of the true giants. They live in rough hillside camps, raiding nearby settlements for food and valuables. Despite their low cunning, they are formidable combatants who can hurl boulders with devastating accuracy and crush foes with their massive greatclubs.

name: Young Red Dragon
summary: A fearsome young dragon with a devastating fire breath.
description: Young red dragons are arrogant, territorial predators who claim large swaths of mountainous terrain as their domains. They breathe cones of searing flame, have a frightful presence that can paralyze lesser creatures with fear, and are protected by thick, fire-resistant scales. Even at a young age, they are among the most dangerous creatures an adventuring party may encounter.

name: Lich
summary: An undead spellcaster who achieved immortality through dark rituals.
description: Liches are powerful undead spellcasters who have used dark necromantic rituals to bind their souls to physical objects called soul cages, achieving a form of immortality. They retain all the magical knowledge and power they possessed in life, augmented by undead resilience. A lich can only be permanently destroyed by first finding and destroying its soul cage.

name: Ancient Red Dragon
summary: A legendary wyrm of immense power, wreathed in flame and terror.
description: Ancient red dragons are among the most powerful creatures in existence. These colossal wyrms have spent centuries accumulating vast hoards of treasure and magical knowledge. Their breath weapon can incinerate entire battalions, their frightful presence can scatter armies, and their mastery of fire magic makes them nearly unstoppable. They are supremely intelligent and view all other creatures as either servants or prey.
